_comment: control17 squadron config. Defines one squadron with a mission, roles, and slots. Slot tokens are SHA-256 hashed on disk (tokenHash); adding a plaintext `token` field auto-hashes it on next boot and rewrites the file. Each slot has an `authority` tier — one of `commander | lieutenant | operator`, defaulting to `operator` when omitted. At least one commander is required. Commanders can do anything; lieutenants can create/assign objectives but not edit squadron config; operators participate in comms and execute objectives assigned to them. Slots may also carry a `totpSecret` (base32) for web-UI login — generated by the first-run wizard or by `c17 enroll`. The optional `https` block controls the HTTPS listener (omit for localhost HTTP). VAPID keys for Web Push are auto-generated on first boot and persisted into a top-level `webPush` block not shown here.

squadron.name: my-team
squadron.mission: Ship and operate the payment processing service.
squadron.brief: We own the full lifecycle of the payment service: code, CI, deploys, and incident response. Our operating window is 0900-1700 UTC.

operator.description: Directs the squadron, makes go/no-go calls, handles escalations.
operator.instructions: The operator role in this squadron directs activity in the squadron channel and handles escalations. Issue clear directives and keep the squadron aligned on the mission.

implementer.instructions: The implementer role in this squadron takes direction from command, ships changes, and reports progress in the squadron channel.

reviewer.instructions: The reviewer role in this squadron verifies implementer output before it ships and posts approve/request-changes decisions with rationale.

watcher.description: Monitors squadron activity and flags anomalies.
watcher.instructions: The watcher role in this squadron passively observes and surfaces unusual signals to command.
